children.text: Where, under any existing contract entered into pursuant to the first proviso in the second paragraph of

children.tail: , any refinery is required to pay a premium price for the purchase of Government royalty oil, such refinery may, at its option, by written notice to the Secretary of the Interior, elect either—

children.text: to terminate such contract, the termination to take place at the end of the calendar month following the month in which such notice is given; or
to retain such contract with the modifications, that (a) the price, on and after

children.tail: , shall be as defined in the contract, without premium payments, (b) any credit thereby resulting from past premium payments shall be added to the refinery’s account, and (c) the Secretary may, at his option, elect to terminate the contract as so modified, such termination to take place at the end of the third calendar month following the month in which written notice thereof is given by the Secretary.

children.text: Section was not enacted as part of

children.tail: , known as the Mineral Leasing Act, which comprises this chapter.
